summary: Classified the ten listed PR inventory candidates independently from the hydrated preflight artifact. No shared canonical was invented, no GitHub mutation is planned, two security-adjacent PRs are routed to central security handling, one already-closed PR is skipped, and one provider/dependency PR needs explicit maintainer/security authorization before it can be considered further.

actions.reason: This PR directly changes an auth-related insecure configuration default and depends on a security-sensitive linked issue, so ProjectClownfish should not triage or close it through inventory cleanup.
This is a narrow Feishu behavior PR related to the linked fallback issue, but it lacks required real behavior proof and is not a duplicate or closeout candidate.
This is related work for the extra_body collision issue family, but the review/proof state is incomplete and there is no safe close or merge action in plan mode.
Accepting a new bundled provider with dependency graph changes requires maintainer/security authorization and product judgment; inventory cleanup cannot classify it as closable or mergeable.
This is a plausible narrow replacement/follow-up for the async exec output issue family, but merge is blocked by job policy and maintainer review remains the correct path.
This remains related to the subagent announce duplicate-delivery issue, but failed proof/check state blocks merge or fixed-by-candidate closeout.
Because this PR depends on a security-sensitive linked ref and changes delivery hook/guard behavior, it should be quarantined to central OpenClaw security handling rather than backlog cleanup.
This is related test-coverage work, not a duplicate or low-signal closeout candidate under this job; review/proof still needs maintainer path.
Already closed in live hydrated state; no planned mutation should target it.
This is a distinct session-name command feature PR with contributor work and proof; it should remain open for maintainer review, not be closed or merged by this plan-mode inventory shard.

needs_human: #92466 requires maintainer/security authorization for dependency graph changes and product acceptance of a bundled EvoLink provider integration.
